# agentd API

> Multi-agent thread daemon. Manages concurrent coding agent threads with persistent conversations, tool use, and real-time streaming.

Base URL: `http://localhost:4747`

## concepts

A **thread** is a persistent conversation with a coding agent. Each thread has its own working directory, model configuration, and message history. Threads persist to disk and survive server restarts.

Sending a message returns immediately (202). The agent's response streams asynchronously via SSE. The agent can read/write/edit files, run bash commands, and search code within the thread's working directory.

## endpoints

### list threads

```
GET /api/threads
```

Returns an array of thread summaries sorted by most recently updated.

Response:
```json
[
  {
    "id": "uuid",
    "title": "My Thread",
    "cwd": "/path/to/project",
    "provider": "anthropic",
    "model": "claude-opus-4-20250514",
    "thinkingLevel": "off",
    "createdAt": "2025-01-01T00:00:00.000Z",
    "updatedAt": "2025-01-01T00:00:00.000Z",
    "status": "idle"
  }
]
```

`status` is either `"idle"` or `"streaming"`.

### create thread

```
POST /api/threads
Content-Type: application/json
```

Body (all fields optional):
```json
{
  "title": "My Thread",
  "cwd": "/path/to/project",
  "provider": "anthropic",
  "model": "claude-opus-4-20250514",
  "thinkingLevel": "high"
}
```

Defaults: title = "New Thread", cwd = server's working directory, provider = "anthropic", model = "claude-opus-4-20250514".

Returns 201 with the thread metadata.

### get thread

```
GET /api/threads/:id
```

Returns thread metadata, status, and full message history.

Response:
```json
{
  "id": "uuid",
  "title": "My Thread",
  "cwd": "/path/to/project",
  "provider": "anthropic",
  "model": "claude-opus-4-20250514",
  "createdAt": "2025-01-01T00:00:00.000Z",
  "updatedAt": "2025-01-01T00:00:00.000Z",
  "status": "idle",
  "messages": [
    { "role": "user", "content": "Hello" },
    { "role": "assistant", "content": [{ "type": "text", "text": "Hi!" }] }
  ]
}
```

Messages follow the Anthropic API message format. Assistant messages have content as an array of blocks (text, tool_use, thinking). Tool results appear as separate messages with role "toolResult".

### delete thread

```
DELETE /api/threads/:id
```

Aborts any running agent, disposes resources, and removes the thread's data directory. Returns `{ "ok": true }`.

### send message

```
POST /api/threads/:id/messages
Content-Type: application/json
```

Body:
```json
{
  "content": "Refactor the auth module to use JWT"
}
```

Returns 202 immediately. The agent processes the message asynchronously. If the agent is already streaming, the message is queued and sent after the current response completes.

Monitor the response via the SSE events endpoint.

### abort

```
POST /api/threads/:id/abort
```

Aborts the agent's current run. Returns `{ "ok": true }`.

### stream events (SSE)

```
GET /api/threads/:id/events
```

Server-Sent Events stream. Connect with EventSource:

```js
const es = new EventSource("/api/threads/{id}/events");
es.addEventListener("snapshot", (e) => { /* ... */ });
es.addEventListener("message_update", (e) => { /* ... */ });
```

Each SSE event has a named `event` type and JSON `data`.

#### event types

**snapshot** — sent immediately on connect
```json
{
  "messages": [...],
  "status": "idle",
  "metadata": { "id": "...", "title": "...", ... }
}
```

**agent_start** — agent begins processing
```json
{}
```

**message_update** — streaming text delta from the assistant
```json
{
  "message": { "role": "assistant", "content": [...] },
  "assistantMessageEvent": { "type": "text_delta", "delta": "partial text" }
}
```

**tool_execution_start** — agent invokes a tool
```json
{
  "toolCallId": "id",
  "toolName": "edit",
  "args": { "file": "src/auth.ts", ... }
}
```

**tool_execution_end** — tool execution completed
```json
{
  "toolCallId": "id",
  "toolName": "edit",
  "isError": false
}
```

**message_end** — assistant message finalized
```json
{
  "message": { "role": "assistant", "content": [...] }
}
```

**agent_end** — agent finished processing (back to idle)
```json
{}
```

**ping** — keepalive every 15 seconds
```json
{}
```

## example: full workflow

```sh
# 1. create a thread
curl -X POST http://localhost:4747/api/threads \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -d '{"title": "auth refactor", "cwd": "/home/user/myproject"}'
# → {"id":"abc-123", ...}

# 2. start streaming events (in another terminal)
curl -N http://localhost:4747/api/threads/abc-123/events
# → event: snapshot
# → data: {"messages":[],"status":"idle","metadata":{...}}

# 3. send a message
curl -X POST http://localhost:4747/api/threads/abc-123/messages \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -d '{"content": "Add input validation to the login endpoint"}'
# → 202 {"ok":true}

# the SSE stream will emit:
#   event: agent_start
#   event: message_update (repeated, with text deltas)
#   event: tool_execution_start (if agent uses tools)
#   event: tool_execution_end
#   event: message_end
#   event: agent_end

# 4. abort if needed
curl -X POST http://localhost:4747/api/threads/abc-123/abort

# 5. delete when done
curl -X DELETE http://localhost:4747/api/threads/abc-123
```

## available tools

Each thread's agent has access to these coding tools, scoped to the thread's working directory:

- **bash** — execute shell commands
- **read** — read file contents
- **write** — write/create files
- **edit** — apply targeted edits to files

The agent decides which tools to use based on the conversation. Tool executions are reported via SSE events so clients can show progress.
